encounter_id.name: encounter_id
encounter_id.cleaning_explanation: This is a unique identifier. Keeping it would cause data leakage or overfitting as it has no predictive clinical value.

patient_nbr.name: patient_nbr
patient_nbr.cleaning_explanation: This is a unique identifier. Keeping it would cause data leakage or overfitting as it has no predictive clinical value.

race.name: race
race.description: Values: Caucasian, Asian, African American, Hispanic, and other.
race.cleaning_explanation: Categorical feature with missing values. Best to fill with the mode, or create a distinct 'Unknown' class if missingness itself is predictive.

gender.name: gender
gender.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard and prevents the model from assuming ordinality.

age.name: age
age.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard and prevents the model from assuming ordinality.

weight.name: weight
weight.cleaning_explanation: Feature has 96.86% missing values. Imputation is statistically unreliable at this threshold. Safe to drop.

admission_type_id.name: admission_type_id
admission_type_id.description: Integer identifier corresponding to 9 distinct values, for example, emergency, urgent, elective, newborn, and not available.
admission_type_id.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

discharge_disposition_id.name: discharge_disposition_id
discharge_disposition_id.description: Integer identifier corresponding to 29 distinct values, for example, discharged to home, expired, and not available.
discharge_disposition_id.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

admission_source_id.name: admission_source_id
admission_source_id.description: Integer identifier corresponding to 21 distinct values, for example, physician referral, emergency room, and transfer from a hospital.
admission_source_id.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

time_in_hospital.name: time_in_hospital
time_in_hospital.description: Integer number of days between admission and discharge.
time_in_hospital.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

payer_code.name: payer_code
payer_code.description: Integer identifier corresponding to 23 distinct values, for example, Blue Cross/Blue Shield, Medicare, and self-pay.
payer_code.cleaning_explanation: Categorical feature with missing values. Best to fill with the mode, or create a distinct 'Unknown' class if missingness itself is predictive.

medical_specialty.name: medical_specialty
medical_specialty.description: Integer identifier of a specialty of the admitting physician, corresponding to 84 distinct values.
medical_specialty.cleaning_explanation: Feature has 49.08% missing values. Imputation is statistically unreliable at this threshold. Safe to drop.

num_lab_procedures.name: num_lab_procedures
num_lab_procedures.description: Number of lab tests performed during the encounter.
num_lab_procedures.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

num_procedures.name: num_procedures
num_procedures.description: Number of procedures (other than lab tests) performed during the encounter.
num_procedures.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

num_medications.name: num_medications
num_medications.description: Number of distinct generic names administered during the encounter.
num_medications.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

number_outpatient.name: number_outpatient
number_outpatient.description: Number of outpatient visits of the patient in the year preceding the encounter.
number_outpatient.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

number_emergency.name: number_emergency
number_emergency.description: Number of emergency visits of the patient in the year preceding the encounter.
number_emergency.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

number_inpatient.name: number_inpatient
number_inpatient.description: Number of inpatient visits of the patient in the year preceding the encounter.
number_inpatient.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

diag_1.name: diag_1
diag_1.description: The primary diagnosis (coded as first three digits of ICD9).
diag_1.cleaning_explanation: High cardinality ICD-9 codes. Recommended to group them into ~9 broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory) as per Strack et al. before encoding.

diag_2.name: diag_2
diag_2.description: Secondary diagnosis (coded as first three digits of ICD9).
diag_2.cleaning_explanation: High cardinality ICD-9 codes. Recommended to group them into ~9 broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory) as per Strack et al. before encoding.

diag_3.name: diag_3
diag_3.description: Additional secondary diagnosis (coded as first three digits of ICD9).
diag_3.cleaning_explanation: High cardinality ICD-9 codes. Recommended to group them into ~9 broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory) as per Strack et al. before encoding.

number_diagnoses.name: number_diagnoses
number_diagnoses.description: Number of diagnoses entered to the system.
number_diagnoses.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after applying StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler to ensure distance-based models perform well.

max_glu_serum.name: max_glu_serum
max_glu_serum.description: Indicates the range of the result or if the test was not taken. Values: '>200', '>300', 'normal', and 'none'.
max_glu_serum.cleaning_explanation: Feature has 94.75% missing values. Imputation is statistically unreliable at this threshold. Safe to drop.

A1Cresult.name: A1Cresult
A1Cresult.description: Indicates the range of the result or if the test was not taken. Values: '>8', '>7', 'normal', and 'none'.
A1Cresult.cleaning_explanation: Feature has 83.28% missing values. Imputation is statistically unreliable at this threshold. Safe to drop.

metformin.name: metformin
metformin.description: Indicates whether the drug metformin was prescribed or there was a change in the dosage. Values: 'up', 'down', 'steady', and 'no'.
metformin.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard and prevents the model from assuming ordinality.

readmitted.name: readmitted
readmitted.description: Target Variable: Days to inpatient readmission. Values: '<30' if the patient was readmitted in less than 30 days, '>30' if the patient was readmitted in more than 30 days, and 'No' for no record of readmission.
readmitted.cleaning_explanation: This is the target variable. Depending on the goal, convert to binary (e.g., '<30' vs 'NO' or '>30') for binary classification.
